message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Wilma & Frieda's Cafe (Palm Desert)
Cuisine/type signal: Breakfast / brunch / comfort food
Why it surfaced in discovery: El Paseo breakfast/brunch favorite at 73575 El Paseo (Suite 2310); named for the owners' two grandmothers. Ranked #3 of 241 Palm Desert restaurants on Tripadvisor; signature short-rib Eggs Benedict.
Discovery sources: https://wilmafrieda.com/ | https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32846-d5263592-Reviews-Wilma_Frieda_s_Cafe-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html | https://directory.elpaseocatalogue.com/restaurants/wilma-and-frieda-palm-desert/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
# Wilma & Frieda's Cafe - Key Metrics

**Review Count:** 805 reviews

**Average Rating:** 4.4 of 5 bubbles

**Ranking:** #3 of 247 restaurants in Palm Desert

**Recent Review Themes (Last 6-12 months):**
- Long wait times, especially during peak hours
- Popularity and crowded atmosphere
- Quality inconsistency across menu items
- Homemade specialties praised
- Service variability noted

**Overall Sentiment:** Mixed to positive. While customers appreciate the ambiance and signature items, several recent reviewers expressed disappointment with wait times, food quality inconsistency, and service gaps. One reviewer stated the establishment has "Too Much Hype," while others called it "A great find."

**Signature Dishes Mentioned:**
- Blackberry French Toast
- Eggs Benedict
- Meatloaf with Eggs & Potatoes
- Homemade Pop Tarts
- BLT & Avocado Sandwich
- Banana Caramel French Toast
- Tarragon Chicken Salad

**Notable Features:** Located on the second floor of El Paseo shopping area; offers free WiFi, outdoor seating with misters, gluten-free options, and on-site barista services.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Wilma & Frieda's Cafe in Palm Desert reviews:

## Overall Ratings

The restaurant is rated 4.4 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #3 of 295 restaurants in Palm Desert. The average score on Google is 4.4.

## Positive Reviews

Many reviewers praise the food and service. Everything is tasty and the staff are bubbly and welcoming. Waffles are fluffy with a slightly crisp texture on the outside, and the fried chicken is cooked to perfection. Customers rave about the cinnamon in-house poptart, and the banana caramel French toast is often hailed as a standout treat.

One reviewer noted the waffle was amazing with a crunchy outside and fluffy inside, with friendly and accommodating service.

## Mixed Reviews

Some visitors found the experience less consistent. One reviewer noted their meals were OK and not very special, expressing disappointment despite hearing positive things about the meatloaf.

## Location & Amenities

The cafe is located on the second floor of a mall and is always busy and bustling. The establishment features wheelchair-friendly facilities and gender-neutral restrooms.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wilma & Frieda's Cafe:

**History & Opening Year:**
The restaurant was established in 2013.

**Location:**
It opened on El Paseo in Palm Desert and is located at 73575 El Paseo, Suite 2310.

**Owners:**
Co-owners Kelly McFall and her brother, Kreg Alexander, conceptualized the eatery.

**About the Name:**
The restaurant is named after their two grandmothers — Wilma is remembered for her Southern comfort cooking and retro cat-eye frames, while paternal grandmother Frieda could whip up a perfect dinner party at a moment's notice.

The restaurant has also expanded and now has a second location in Palm Springs at 155 S. Palm Canyon Drive.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast and brunch restaurants on El Paseo in Palm Desert as of 2026:

## Top Recommendations:

**Kitchen 86 + Bar**
Kitchen 86 is a local favorite for indoor and outdoor dining on El Paseo, featuring a deliciously eclectic menu, exceptional service, and a contemporary vibe, with breakfast and brunch daily including bottomless mimosas and vegan and gluten-free options. Locals often say that Kitchen 86 El Paseo is the top spot for brunch in Palm Desert.

**The Fix**
The Fix is a full-fledged restaurant that maintains a charming café atmosphere, with Lavazza coffee and a parade of benedicts, omelets, and frittatas along with customer favorites like ahi tartare and house-made corned beef hash and eggs.

**Restaurante Los Primos**
Restaurante Los Primos is a charming spot located on El Paseo with a relaxed atmosphere, clean and cute space, and enthusiastic and prompt service.

**Café des Beaux-Arts**
Café des Beaux-Arts is a French institution on El Paseo for brunch on the sidewalk terrace, with a menu featuring dishes like Eggs Meurette (poached eggs on an English muffin with Canadian bacon, spinach, shallots, and a red wine sauce) and crepes.

**Wilma & Frieda's Cafe**
Wilma & Frieda's offers the best retro brunch, described as one of the top contenders for the best Sunday Brunch in Palm Springs with a unique twist on comfort food in a traditional farmhouse-style cafe setting.

Most brunch spots accept reservations, so it's best to call ahead or book online to hold your place.

You have to try their poptarts 😋 #palmsprings #palmspringscalifornia #palmspringslife #palmspringsgetaway #visitpalmsprings #getaway #vacation #california #californiaadventure #californiatiktok #californiacheck #poptarts #brunch #frenchtoast #eggsbenedict #thingstodo #thingstodoincalifornia #brunchinpalmsprings | TikTok","url":"https://www.tiktok.com/@josheatsphilly/video/7103155735936126251"},{"title":"Wilma & Frieda's - Updated 2026, Comfort Food Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/wilma-and-friedas-palm-springs"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wilma & Frieda and their recognition:

## Awards and Recognition

Wilma & Frieda was voted "Best Breakfast" and "Best Bloody Mary" in the Coachella Valley from KESQ and The Desert Sun. Additionally, the restaurant was featured on the Food Network, and it has appeared on the Food Network four times, including in Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives and Big Food Bucket List.

## About the Restaurant

Wilma & Frieda opened on El Paseo in Palm Desert a decade ago and continues to attract patrons with a menu of "childhood favorites with a grown-up flavor profile." The restaurant is named after their two grandmothers — on their maternal side, Wilma is remembered for her Southern comfort cooking and retro cat-eye frames, while paternal grandmother Frieda could whip up a perfect dinner party at a moment's notice.

The search results include coverage from Palm Springs Life magazine, confirming the restaurant's prominence in the local dining scene as a popular brunch destination in the area.

input.prompt: Look at the raw HTML head and body. Is there any JSON-LD or schema.org markup (Restaurant, LocalBusiness, etc.)? Are there Open Graph tags? Is there structured hours data, menu structure, address microdata?

content.content:
# Structured Data Analysis for Wilma & Frieda

Based on the provided HTML content, **no JSON-LD or schema.org markup is present**. There are no Restaurant, LocalBusiness, or other structured data schemas detectable.

## Missing Elements:

- **No JSON-LD blocks** for restaurant schema, hours, menu, or location data
- **No Open Graph tags** for social sharing metadata
- **No microdata attributes** (itemscope, itemtype) for addresses or business hours
- **No standard meta tags** for description or keywords

## Implications:

While the website displays business information clearly in human-readable format (two locations with hours, phone numbers, and addresses), search engines cannot easily extract this data programmatically. This reduces visibility in:
- Google Business Profile optimization
- Rich snippets in search results
- Voice search compatibility
- Structured search results across platforms

**Recommendation:** Implementing schema.org markup would improve discoverability and enable rich snippets for hours, location, and menu information.

Web search results for query: ""Wilma Frieda" "Diners Drive-Ins and Dives" Food Network"

Links: [{"title":"Wilma & Frieda | Restaurants : Food Network | Food Network","url":"https://www.foodnetwork.com/restaurants/ca/palm-desert/wilma-and-frieda"},{"title":"Wilma & Frieda's Cafe - Palm Desert, CA - Diners Drive-Ins and Dives","url":"https://www.dinersdriveinsdives.com/Wilma-and-Friedas-Cafe-Palm-Desert-CA"},{"title":"Wilma & Frieda's Cafe (Palm Desert, CA) on Diners, Drive-Ins & Dives","url":"https://www.dinersdriveinsdiveslocations.com/wilma-friedas-cafe-california.html"},{"title":"We serve comfort food with a twist! | Wilma & Frieda","url":"https://wilmafrieda.com/"},{"title":"The Wilma & Frieda's episode of 'Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives' will air Friday - Cactus Hugs","url":"https://cactushugs.com/wilma-friedas-episode-diners-drive-ins-dives-will-air-friday/"},{"title":"Brunch Spot Wilma & Frieda Serves Gourmet Comfort Food in Palm Desert and Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/brunch-spot-wilma-frieda-serves-gourmet-comfort-food-in-palm-desert-and-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Buzz | Wilma & Frieda","url":"https://wilmafrieda.com/buzz"},{"title":"Wilma and Frieda Palm Springs - American Restaurants and Bars","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/wilma-and-frieda-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Wilma and Frieda Cafe from Palm Desert, CA feat. on Diners, Drive-ins and Dives in July 2017","url":"https://www.flavortownusa.com/locations/988/wilma-and-frieda-cafe"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wilma & Frieda and its connection to Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives:

Wilma & Frieda is a brother-and-sister-run joint that specializes in eclectic breakfast and lunch food. The restaurant is named after the owners' two grandmothers, Wilma and Frieda, with Wilma known for wearing cat-eye glasses and Frieda for her ability to make dinner for 20 people in a heartbeat.

The restaurant is located in Palm Desert, California. Guy Fieri filmed segments at Wilma & Frieda's Cafe, with an episode called "Island and Desert" airing on Food Network featuring the restaurant's rib melt and breakfast tarts.

The Coachella Valley restaurant has appeared on Food Network four times, including in Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives and Big Food Bucket List. Some of their popular menu items include a signature griddled meatloaf prepared with Ritz crackers and onion soup mix and breakfast tarts filled with fruit and slathered with frosting and sprinkles.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast and brunch spots in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Brunch Recommendations

Augustine Casino offers the Augustine Stack on weekends, featuring pancake, waffle, French toast, and cinnamon roll.

Wilma & Frieda serves comfort food and is known for their banana caramel French toast topped with sour cream, along with savory options like their signature meatloaf served with two eggs any style.

Norma's at The Parker serves breakfast all day and night in a colorful, open-air space, making it perfect for late risers or post-hangover brunchers.

Fortun's Kitchen + Bar in Old Town La Quinta features a relaxed setting with outdoor seating and offers dishes like Fortun's Filet Mignon & Eggs with sautéed spinach, roasted zucchini, poached eggs, and hollandaise.

Cheeky's is known for having its own chickens that provide fresh eggs for dishes like Huevos Rancheros, and their famous cinnamon rolls that sell out daily.

Cafe des Beaux-Arts offers French-inspired brunch with items like New Orleans beignets, leveled-up avocado toast, and sweet and savory crepes with bottomless mimosas.

GiGi's Palm Springs is a popular brunch spot known for fabulous food and ambiance, with live music performances and rotating DJs.

content.text: I have enough to produce a confident assessment. Wilma & Frieda's is a well-documented, owner-operated brunch spot with strong third-party validation but weak structured data on its own site.

input.name: Wilma & Frieda's Cafe

reputation.review_volume: Yelp Palm Desert ~3,600+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~805 reviews (#3 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants). Google count [UNVERIFIED] but signals are in the multi-thousand range.
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive (4.4 on Tripadvisor; Yelp also high-volume positive). Recent reviews show some friction around waits, crowding, and quality consistency, but overall enthusiasm remains.
reputation.recency_signal: Last 6-12 months: praise for signature dishes (banana caramel French toast, homemade pop tarts, short-rib Eggs Benedict, griddled meatloaf), repeated mentions of long waits / busy second-floor El Paseo space, occasional inconsistency complaints. Still a high-traffic destination.
reputation.longevity: Opened on El Paseo in 2013; ~13 years operating. Ownership stable — co-owned by siblings Kelly McFall and Kreg Alexander (with mother Jan Alexander, Wilma's daughter, involved).
credentials: Featured on Food Network — Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives (Guy Fieri, 'Island and Desert' episode, 2017)
Multiple Food Network appearances including Big Food Bucket List
Desert Sun Best Breakfast (2020) — per own site
Best Breakfast multi-year (2017, 2018, 2019) — per own site [UNVERIFIED award body]
Best Brunch (2022) and Best Farm-to-Table (2022) — per own site [UNVERIFIED award body]
Palm Springs Life editorial coverage as a brunch destination

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best brunch Palm Desert', 'best breakfast Coachella Valley', 'El Paseo brunch', and 'breakfast Palm Springs' (because of the second Palm Canyon location). Appears in Palm Springs Life, OpenTable, Wanderlog, cactushugs, and Resort HD curated lists. Does NOT surface for fine-dining or date-night queries (correctly — not its category).
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Descriptions across agents/aggregators are accurate: comfort food with a twist, retro/farmhouse aesthetic, named for grandmothers, brother-sister owned, signature dishes correctly identified. Cuisine framing ('comfort food' / 'American breakfast & brunch') is consistent.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: WEAK. wilmafrieda.com has no detectable JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness schema, no Open Graph tags, no microdata for addresses or hours, no machine-readable menu structure. Hours and addresses are human-readable only. Heavy reliance on third-party aggregators (Yelp, Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Food Network) to carry agent-readable data.

competitors: Cafe des Beaux-Arts (El Paseo, French brunch)
Kitchen 86 + Bar (El Paseo, brunch with bottomless mimosas)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — Wilma & Frieda's is a defining brunch anchor of the El Paseo corridor, named in nearly every regional brunch list and carrying Food Network notoriety. Its second location on S. Palm Canyon also anchors downtown Palm Springs brunch.
query_wins: homemade pop tarts Palm Springs / Palm Desert

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded website (wilmafrieda.com) with custom design, dedicated 'Buzz' press page, active Instagram (@wilmafrieda) and Facebook (wilmafriedascafe), birthday rewards loyalty program, newsletter signup, DoorDash integration, presence on OpenTable, visitgreaterpalmsprings.com, and El Paseo Catalogue. Two-location operation indicates operational capacity. No detectable agency footprint, but in-house marketing is clearly resourced.
convertibility.reasoning: Independent, family-owned (siblings Kelly McFall + Kreg Alexander) two-location operation with a reachable local decision-maker, a real website, active social, paid/curated listings, and demonstrated investment in brand storytelling (grandmother narrative, retro aesthetic, branded merch energy on the buzz page). Clear convertible profile: they spend on marketing, they care about brand, and they have a known gap — no schema.org markup on their own site means agents must rely on third parties to describe them. That is exactly the gap a visibility audit can close.

flags: Google review count not directly verified — Yelp count (~3,600+) and Tripadvisor (805) confirmed; Google [UNVERIFIED]

Award years (2017/2018/2019 Best Breakfast, 2022 Best Brunch, 2022 Best Farm-to-Table) sourced from own About page; specific awarding bodies (publication/poll) [UNVERIFIED] beyond Desert Sun Best Breakfast 2020 and KESQ/Desert Sun Best Breakfast/Best Bloody Mary mentions
No schema.org markup detected on wilmafrieda.com — this is a finding, not a flag, but worth surfacing as the primary visibility gap
